name: Alzheimer's Disease
type:LABEL: Disease
description: Most common cause of dementia worldwide; biologically defined by amyloid plaques and neurofibrillary tangles

name: Dementia
type:LABEL: Disease
description: Umbrella syndrome of acquired cognitive decline affecting daily function

name: Vascular Dementia
type:LABEL: Disease
description: Second most common dementia subtype, driven by cerebrovascular disease

name: Mixed Dementia
type:LABEL: Disease
description: Co-occurrence of more than one dementia pathology, most often AD plus vascular dementia

name: Mild Cognitive Impairment
type:LABEL: Disease
description: Prodromal cognitive decline stage; amnestic MCI often precedes AD

name: Down Syndrome
type:LABEL: Disease
description: Trisomy 21 condition; extra APP gene copy links it to the amyloid hypothesis

name: Amyloid-beta (Abeta)
type:LABEL: Protein
description: Peptide fragment of APP that aggregates into amyloid plaques; core AD biomarker

name: Amyloid Precursor Protein / APP gene
type:LABEL: Protein_Gene
description: Transmembrane protein cleaved to release amyloid-beta; gene on chromosome 21

name: Tau Protein
type:LABEL: Protein
description: Microtubule-stabilizing protein; hyperphosphorylated form aggregates into tangles

name: Neurofibrillary Tangles
type:LABEL: Pathology
description: Intracellular aggregates of hyperphosphorylated tau; hallmark AD pathology

name: Amyloid Plaques
type:LABEL: Pathology
description: Extracellular aggregates of amyloid-beta fibrils; hallmark AD pathology

name: Apolipoprotein E (APOE)
type:LABEL: Protein_Gene
description: Lipid-transport protein/gene; allele status is the major genetic risk modifier for sporadic AD

name: APOE epsilon-4 allele
type:LABEL: GeneVariant
description: Strongest common genetic risk factor for sporadic AD

name: APOE epsilon-2 allele
type:LABEL: GeneVariant
description: Strongest common genetic protective factor against AD

name: Presenilin 1 (PSEN1)
type:LABEL: Gene
description: Gene on chromosome 14; mutations cause autosomal-dominant familial AD

name: Presenilin 2 (PSEN2)
type:LABEL: Gene
description: Gene on chromosome 1; mutations cause autosomal-dominant familial AD

name: Chromosome 21
type:LABEL: Chromosome
description: Carries the APP gene; triplicated in Down syndrome

name: Chromosome 14
type:LABEL: Chromosome
description: Carries the PSEN1 gene

name: Chromosome 1
type:LABEL: Chromosome
description: Carries the PSEN2 gene

name: Hippocampus
type:LABEL: BrainRegion
description: Brain region for memory formation; earliest major atrophy site in AD

name: Microglia
type:LABEL: CellType
description: Brain innate-immune cells; mediate neuroinflammation and interact with Abeta and tau

name: Neuroinflammation
type:LABEL: Mechanism
description: Innate immune activation implicated in AD pathogenesis, partly independent of amyloid

name: Glymphatic System
type:LABEL: Mechanism
description: Brain waste-clearance system, most active during sleep; degrades with age

name: Sleep Disturbance
type:LABEL: RiskFactor
description: Impaired sleep architecture is a frequent antecedent of dementia onset

name: Synaptic Dysfunction / Loss
type:LABEL: Mechanism
description: Loss of synaptic homeostasis central to AD-associated cognitive decline

name: Amyloid Cascade Hypothesis
type:LABEL: Theory
description: Hypothesis that amyloid-beta accumulation triggers downstream tau pathology and neurodegeneration

name: NMDA Receptor
type:LABEL: MolecularTarget
description: Glutamate receptor; pathological overstimulation implicated in excitotoxic neurodegeneration

name: Glutamate Excitotoxicity
type:LABEL: Mechanism
description: Pathological NMDA receptor overstimulation by glutamate, hypothesized to contribute to AD

name: Acetylcholine
type:LABEL: Neurotransmitter
description: Neurotransmitter reduced in the AD brain; target of cholinesterase inhibitor therapy

name: Cholinesterase Inhibitors
type:LABEL: DrugClass
description: Main symptomatic AD drug class; raises synaptic acetylcholine

name: Donepezil
type:LABEL: Drug
description: Reversible cholinesterase inhibitor used for symptomatic AD treatment

name: Rivastigmine
type:LABEL: Drug
description: Pseudo-irreversible cholinesterase inhibitor used for AD

name: Galantamine
type:LABEL: Drug
description: Reversible cholinesterase inhibitor, less well tolerated than donepezil

name: Tacrine
type:LABEL: Drug
description: First licensed cholinesterase inhibitor; withdrawn due to hepatotoxicity

name: Hepatotoxicity
type:LABEL: AdverseEffect
description: Liver toxicity that led to tacrine's discontinuation

name: Memantine
type:LABEL: Drug
description: NMDA receptor antagonist used for moderate-to-severe AD

name: Anti-Amyloid Monoclonal Antibodies
type:LABEL: DrugClass
description: Disease-modifying antibody class that clears brain amyloid-beta

name: Lecanemab
type:LABEL: Drug
description: FDA-approved (2023) anti-amyloid antibody; slows cognitive decline, can cause ARIA

name: Aducanumab
type:LABEL: Drug
description: FDA accelerated-approval anti-amyloid antibody for early AD

name: Amyloid-Related Imaging Abnormalities (ARIA)
type:LABEL: AdverseEffect
description: Brain edema/microhemorrhage side effect of anti-amyloid antibody therapy

name: CSF / Plasma Biomarkers (Abeta42, tau)
type:LABEL: Biomarker
description: Cerebrospinal fluid or plasma amyloid-beta and tau levels used for AD diagnosis

name: Amyloid PET Imaging
type:LABEL: Biomarker
description: In-vivo imaging technique that detects brain amyloid plaque burden

name: Tau PET Imaging
type:LABEL: Biomarker
description: Imaging technique for tau pathology; aids differential diagnosis and trial selection

name: Structural MRI (atrophy)
type:LABEL: Biomarker
description: Detects hippocampal and cortical atrophy for diagnosis and disease tracking

name: Physical Activity
type:LABEL: ProtectiveFactor
description: Associated with a meaningfully lower risk of AD and all-cause dementia

name: Cardiovascular / Vascular Risk Factors
type:LABEL: RiskFactor
description: Hypertension and vascular disease raise dementia and AD risk

name: Air Pollution (particulate matter)
type:LABEL: RiskFactor
description: Fine particulate matter and related pollutants raise dementia and AD risk

name: Aging
type:LABEL: RiskFactor
description: The single greatest risk factor for AD and dementia generally

name: Family History / Genetic Predisposition
type:LABEL: RiskFactor
description: A first-degree relative with AD raises personal risk substantially

name: Caregiver Burden
type:LABEL: SocialImpact
description: Indirect caregiving cost is the main driver of AD's societal cost

name: Economic Burden of AD
type:LABEL: SocialImpact
description: Global dementia care cost is projected to reach roughly $2 trillion by 2030

name: Sex / Gender Differences
type:LABEL: ModifyingFactor
description: Biological sex and gender modify AD risk, pathology burden, and resilience

name: Memory Loss
type:LABEL: Symptom
description: Short-term memory loss is typically the earliest noticeable AD symptom

name: Cognitive Decline
type:LABEL: Symptom
description: Progressive decline in cognition and function across AD stages
